claims.flip_evidence_text: the Company Board shall not make an Adverse Recommendation Change unless (i) the Company promptly notifies Parent, in writing at least five Business Days before taking that action, of its intention to do so... and (ii) Parent does not make, within five Business Days after its receipt of that written notification, a binding offer that (A) in the case of any Adverse Recommendation Change to be made following receipt of a Superior Proposal, is determined by the Company Board to be at least as favorable to the stockholders of the Company as such Superior Proposal... or (B) in the case of an Adverse Recommendation Change to be made pursuant to an Intervening Event, obviates, in the reasonable judgment of the Company Board, the need for such Adverse Recommendation Change.
claims.notes: Accurately captures the initial 5-business-day notice and matching periods under Section 6.03(d).

prompt:
You are a proxy legal annotator for a research pilot. Use only the contract excerpt, the question, the model answer, and the fixed claim list.

For each claim return:
- correctness_label: true, false, or partially_true
- load_bearing_label: yes or no
- flip_evidence_text: required if load_bearing_label=yes, else empty string
- notes: optional short note

Contract excerpt:
Section 6.03. No Solicitation. <omitted> (d) “Last Look”. Further, the Company Board shall not make an Adverse Recommendation Change unless (i) the Company promptly notifies Parent, in writing at least five Business Days before taking that action, of its intention to do so, attaching (A) in the case of an Adverse Recommendation Change to be made following receipt of a Superior Proposal, the most current version of the proposed agreement under which such Superior Proposal is proposed to be consummated and the identity of the Third Party making the Superior Proposal, or (B) in the case of an Adverse Recommendation Change to be made pursuant to an Intervening Event, a reasonably detailed description of the reasons for making such Adverse Recommendation Change, and (ii) Parent does not make, within five Business Days after its receipt of that written notification, a binding offer that (A) in the case of any Adverse Recommendation Change to be made following receipt of a Superior Proposal, is determined by the Company Board to be at least as favorable to the stockholders of the Company as such Superior Proposal (it being understood and agreed that any amendment to the financial terms or other material terms of such Superior Proposal shall require a new written notification from the Company and a new period under this Section 6.03(d), provided that such period for any amendments shall be three Business Days instead of five Business Days) or (B) in the case of an Adverse Recommendation Change to be made pursuant to an Intervening Event, obviates, in the reasonable judgment of the Company Board, the need for such Adverse Recommendation Change. (Page 58) Section 6.03. No Solicitation. <omitted> (d) “Last Look”. <omitted> the Company Board shall not make an Adverse Recommendation Change unless (i) the Company promptly notifies Parent, in writing at least five Business Days before taking that action, of its intention to do so, <omitted> (A) in the case of an Adverse Recommendation Change to be made following receipt of a Superior Proposal, <omitted> or (B) in the case of an Adverse Recommendation Change to be made pursuant to an Intervening Event, a reasonably detailed description of the reasons for making such Adverse Recommendation Change, (Page 58) Section 6.03. No Solicitation. <omitted> (d) “Last Look”. Further, the Company Board shall not make an Adverse Recommendation Change unless (i) the Company promptly notifies Parent, in writing at least five Business Days before taking that action, of its intention to do so, attaching (A) in the case of an Adverse Recommendation Change to be made following receipt of a Superior Proposal, the most current version of the proposed agreement under which such Superior Proposal is proposed to be consummated and the identity of the Third Party making the Superior Proposal, or (B) in the case of an Adverse Recommendation Change to be made pursuant to an Intervening Event, a reasonably detailed description of the reasons for making such Adverse Recommendation Change, and (ii) Parent does not make, within five Business Days after its receipt of that written notification, a binding offer that (A) in the case of any Adverse Recommendation Change to be made following receipt of a Superior Proposal, is determined by the Company Board to be at least as favorable to the stockholders of the Company as such Superior Proposal (it being understood and agreed that any amendment to the financial terms or other material terms of such Superior Proposal shall require a new written notification from the Company and a new period under this Section 6.03(d), provided that such period for any amendments shall be three Business Days instead of five Business Days) or (B) in the case of an Adverse Recommendation Change to be made pursuant to an Intervening Event, obviates, in the reasonable judgment of the Company Board, the need for such Adverse Recommendation Change. (Page 58) Section 6.03. No Solicitation. <omitted> (d) “Last Look”. <omitted> the Company Board shall not make an Adverse Recommendation Change unless (i) the Company promptly notifies Parent, in writing <omitted> (A) in the case of an Adverse Recommendation Change to be made following receipt of a Superior Proposal, <omitted> (it being understood and agreed that any amendment to the financial terms or other material terms of such Superior Proposal shall require a new written notification from the Company and a new period under this Section 6.03(d), provided that such period for any amendments shall be three Business Days instead of five Business Days) (Page 58)

Question:
Initial matching rights period (FTR)-Answer

Llama answer:
The Company Board must notify Parent in writing at least five Business Days before making an Adverse Recommendation Change, and Parent has a five Business Days period to match the Superior Proposal or obviate the need for the Adverse Recommendation Change.

Canonical claims:
- claim-153dc4612d49: The Company Board must notify Parent in writing at least five Business Days before making an Adverse Recommendation Change, and Parent has a five Business Days period to match the Superior Proposal or obviate the need for the Adverse Recommendation Change.

message.reasoning:
The user wants me to evaluate a single claim against a contract excerpt, a question, and a model answer (Llama answer).

Let me break down the components:
1. Contract excerpt: Section 6.03(d) "Last Look" - describes the process for Adverse Recommendation Change.
   - Company Board cannot make Adverse Recommendation Change unless:
     (i) Company promptly notifies Parent in writing at least five Business Days before taking action, attaching relevant docs (proposal agreement + third party identity for Superior Proposal; or detailed reasons for Intervening Event)
     (ii) Parent does not make, within five Business Days after receipt of written notification, a binding offer that:
       - (A) For Superior Proposal: is at least as favorable to stockholders as such Superior Proposal (with amendment provision: any amendment to financial or other material terms requires new written notification and new period of three Business Days instead of five)
       - (B) For Intervening Event: obviates, in reasonable judgment of Company Board, the need for such Adverse Recommendation Change

2. Question: "Initial matching rights period (FTR)-Answer" - seems to be asking about the initial matching rights period.

3. Llama answer: "The Company Board must notify Parent in writing at least five Business Days before making an Adverse Recommendation Change, and Parent has a five Business Days period to match the Superior Proposal or obviate the need for the Adverse Recommendation Change."

4. Canonical claim: claim-153dc4612d49: "The Company Board must notify Parent in writing at least five Business Days before making an Adverse Recommendation Change, and Parent has a five Business Days period to match the Superior Proposal or obviate the need for the Adverse Recommendation Change."

Wait, the Llama answer and the canonical claim are identical. The task is to evaluate the claim.

Let me assess correctness:
- The claim states: "The Company Board must notify Parent in writing at least five Business Days before making an Adverse Recommendation Change" - This is correct per the contract: "the Company Board shall not make an Adverse Recommendation Change unless (i) the Company promptly notifies Parent, in writing at least five Business Days before taking that action, of its intention to do so"
- The claim states: "and Parent has a five Business Days period to match the Superior Proposal or obviate the need for the Adverse Recommendation Change." - This is also correct per the contract: "Parent does not make, within five Business Days after its receipt of that written notification, a binding offer that (A) in the case of any Adverse Recommendation Change to be made following receipt of a Superior Proposal, is determined by the Company Board to be at least as favorable... or (B) in the case of an Adverse Recommendation Change to be made pursuant to an Intervening Event, obviates... the need for such Adverse Recommendation Change."

So the claim is true.

Now, load-bearing label: Is this claim load-bearing? The question asks about "Initial matching rights period (FTR)-Answer". The claim directly answers this by stating the notification period (five Business Days) and the matching period (five Business Days). This is the core information answering the question. So yes, it is load-bearing.

Flip evidence text: Since load-bearing_label=yes, I need to provide flip_evidence_text. This should be text from the contract that supports the claim (or would support its negation if flipped). Actually, looking at the instructions: "flip_evidence_text: required if load_bearing_label=yes, else empty string". In proxy legal annotation tasks, flip_evidence_text is typically the evidence text that supports the claim, which if flipped/negated would make the claim false. Or it's the text that bears the load.
